verses.text: 'Being justified by faith, we have peace with God,' and rejoice in hope of His glory. Peace and hope. Justification brings reconciliation and joy.
'Being justified by faith, we have peace with God,' and rejoice in hope of His glory. Peace and hope. Justification brings reconciliation and joy.
We glory in tribulations, which work patience and hope; 'the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts.' Joy in trial. Suffering deepens hope, not despair.
We glory in tribulations, which work patience and hope; 'the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts.' Joy in trial. Suffering deepens hope, not despair.
We glory in tribulations, which work patience and hope; 'the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts.' Joy in trial. Suffering deepens hope, not despair.
'While we were yet sinners, Christ died for us'; God commends His love. Unmerited love. Christ died for us at our worst.
'While we were yet sinners, Christ died for us'; God commends His love. Unmerited love. Christ died for us at our worst.
'While we were yet sinners, Christ died for us'; God commends His love. Unmerited love. Christ died for us at our worst.
Justified by His blood, we are saved from wrath, and 'joy in God' through our Lord. Full salvation. Reconciled, we rejoice in God Himself.
Justified by His blood, we are saved from wrath, and 'joy in God' through our Lord. Full salvation. Reconciled, we rejoice in God Himself.
Justified by His blood, we are saved from wrath, and 'joy in God' through our Lord. Full salvation. Reconciled, we rejoice in God Himself.
By one man sin entered, and death by sin, 'for that all have sinned.' Sin's reign. Adam's fall brought death to all.
By one man sin entered, and death by sin, 'for that all have sinned.' Sin's reign. Adam's fall brought death to all.
By one man sin entered, and death by sin, 'for that all have sinned.' Sin's reign. Adam's fall brought death to all.
But the free gift is far greater; through One 'shall reign in life' the many. Grace abounds. Christ gives more than Adam lost.
But the free gift is far greater; through One 'shall reign in life' the many. Grace abounds. Christ gives more than Adam lost.
But the free gift is far greater; through One 'shall reign in life' the many. Grace abounds. Christ gives more than Adam lost.
As by one offence judgment came on all, so by 'the righteousness of one' the free gift came unto life. The two Adams. One brought ruin, the Other righteousness.
As by one offence judgment came on all, so by 'the righteousness of one' the free gift came unto life. The two Adams. One brought ruin, the Other righteousness.
Where sin abounded, 'grace did much more abound'; grace reigns 'unto eternal life by Jesus Christ.' Grace triumphs. No sin can outrun the grace of God.
